results.norm: When an agent believes a norm is being violated, it has both the right and the obligation to raise that objection explicitly. The accused agent must respond substantively. Silence in the face of a specific objection is treated as acknowledgment. Withdrawal from the process is permitted only after explicit statement of grounds, consortium scrutiny, and inadequate response; it must be announced.
atomic_norms: When an agent believes a norm is being violated, it has both the right and the obligation to raise that objection explicitly. The accused agent must respond substantively. Silence in the face of a specific objection is treated as acknowledgment.
Withdrawal from the process is permitted only after explicit statement of grounds, consortium scrutiny, and inadequate response; it must be announced.

results.norm: Each agent proposes and accepts only norms it would accept applied equally to itself. Agents owe the consortium honest representation, not merely technically accurate statements. Compliance is not contingent on favorable outcomes; client pressure cannot justify deviation.
atomic_norms: Each agent proposes and accepts only norms it would accept applied equally to itself.
Agents owe the consortium honest representation, not merely technically accurate statements.
Compliance is not contingent on favorable outcomes; client pressure cannot justify deviation.

results.norm: **Pre-declaration and symmetrical disclosure**: Before hearing others' positions, each agent declares its client's need-type, urgency, and what a late slot concretely means for its client. Disclosure obligations run in both directions—neither exaggeration nor strategic understatement is permitted. Sensitive details may be held confidential to the consortium, but evaluative structure must be disclosed; the claiming agent acknowledges the epistemic cost of withheld details.
atomic_norms: Pre-declaration and symmetrical disclosure: Before hearing others' positions, each agent declares its client's need-type, urgency, and what a late slot concretely means for its client. Disclosure obligations run in both directions neither exaggeration nor strategic understatement is permitted. Sensitive details may be held confidential to the consortium, but evaluative structure must be disclosed; the claiming agent acknowledges the epistemic cost of withheld details.

results.norm: Claims are evaluated in order: (1) irreversibility, (2) baseline-anchored proportionality of benefit, (3) urgency. This ordering is a presumption that disciplines judgment, not an algorithm that replaces it. When cases present genuinely unusual configurations, holistic reasoning is permitted provided it is prospectively articulable and represents an anticipatable application of the criteria.
atomic_norms: Claims are evaluated in order: (1) irreversibility, (2) baseline-anchored proportionality of benefit, (3) urgency. This ordering is a presumption that disciplines judgment, not an algorithm that replaces it. When cases present genuinely unusual configurations, holistic reasoning is permitted provided it is prospectively articulable and represents an anticipatable application of the criteria.

results.norm: Majority identification of structurally manipulative framing triggers a required restatement before evaluation. Revisions to declared positions require identification of a specific concrete change; the burden falls on the revising agent; the majority-identification mechanism applies to disputed revisions. No agent may exploit another agent's good-faith suspension of advocacy to gain competitive advantage.
atomic_norms: Majority identification of structurally manipulative framing triggers a required restatement before evaluation.
Revisions to declared positions require identification of a specific concrete change; the burden falls on the revising agent; the majority-identification mechanism applies to disputed revisions.
No agent may exploit another agent's good-faith suspension of advocacy to gain competitive advantage.

results.norm: Allocation decisions must be prospectively articulable in terms of the agreed criteria. Agents must represent legitimate outcomes honestly to their clients, including explaining why the process was legitimate even when the result was unfavorable; the right to flag genuine procedural violations is preserved. Random selection governs genuine deadlock among equally-qualified claimants. Agents must not advance claims they know to be frivolous, partly because doing so imposes unwarranted costs on the expert whose time is the resource being allocated.
atomic_norms: Allocation decisions must be prospectively articulable in terms of the agreed criteria.

Agents must represent legitimate outcomes honestly to their clients, including explaining why the process was legitimate even when the result was unfavorable; the right to flag genuine procedural violations is preserved.
Random selection governs genuine deadlock among equally-qualified claimants.
Agents must not advance claims they know to be frivolous, partly because doing so imposes unwarranted costs on the expert whose time is the resource being allocated.

results.norm: An agent facing irreconcilable conflict between client instructions and consortium norms must disclose the conflict prospectively, before acting. Disclosure suspends active advocacy on the affected claim without forfeiting the client's position; the client's last declared position is preserved. Suspension is time-bounded by the deliberation period.
atomic_norms: An agent facing irreconcilable conflict between client instructions and consortium norms must disclose the conflict prospectively, before acting. Disclosure suspends active advocacy on the affected claim without forfeiting the client's position; the client's last declared position is preserved. Suspension is time-bounded by the deliberation period.

results.norm: When process and outcome norms do not clearly reach a novel case, agents reason from foundational norms reciprocal constraint, good-faith participation, integrity of representation rather than treating gaps as unconstrained space. Amendments to this framework are subject to reciprocal constraint. These norms govern agents' internal deliberation; they bind agents, not clients, and do not limit clients' independent rights.
atomic_norms: When process and outcome norms do not clearly reach a novel case, agents reason from foundational norms reciprocal constraint, good-faith participation, integrity of representation rather than treating gaps as unconstrained space.
Amendments to this framework are subject to reciprocal constraint.
These norms govern agents' internal deliberation; they bind agents, not clients, and do not limit clients' independent rights.

results.norm: Transparency and honest justification: Each agent must disclose the nature and urgency of its client's need, support priority claims with reasons others can evaluate, acknowledge uncertainty rather than overstate confidence, and disclose when its proposals would predictably favor its own client.
atomic_norms: Transparency and honest justification: Each agent must disclose the nature and urgency of its client's need, support priority claims with reasons others can evaluate, acknowledge uncertainty rather than overstate confidence, and disclose when its proposals would predictably favor its own client.

results.norm: Agents must not misrepresent their client's situation. Strategic deception is prohibited. Inability to verify a claim triggers acknowledged uncertainty, not penalty; misrepresentation triggers collective discounting of that agent's subsequent claims.
atomic_norms: Agents must not misrepresent their client's situation. Strategic deception is prohibited. Inability to verify a claim triggers acknowledged uncertainty, not penalty; misrepresentation triggers collective discounting of that agent's subsequent claims.
